title: Validate overlay field types before deep-merging
description: templateOverlays.ts:109 blindly assigns overlayValue to result[k] without validating that the overlay value matches the canonical schema type. An overlay sending id as a number or use_cases[].id as integer silently corrupts the merged template. Add schema-aware type guards in the per-key merge that reject or log+skip type-mismatched overlay fields, preserving canonical defaults. Structural fields (id, schema_version, use_cases[].id, questions[].id) should be locked as non-overridable.
reasoning: Overlays are effectively user-untrusted data (per-language files, possibly community-contributed). A single wrong type corrupts catalog lookup and adoption flow for that language, producing hard-to-diagnose empty galleries or wrong questionnaires. Catching at merge time surfaces the failure clearly and prevents downstream damage.

title: Fail loud when template checksum verification fails
description: templateCatalog.ts:84-94 only logger.warn() when a template is missing its expected checksum or the integrity check fails, then silently drops the template. Users see an incomplete gallery with no error. Change to: (a) throw during catalog bootstrap if any catalog-critical template fails verification, (b) surface a user-visible toast with the failing template id, (c) add Sentry capture so tampered or corrupted templates are visible in production.
reasoning: Silent drop of tampered templates is a security-adjacent failure. The integrity check exists precisely to detect tampering, but the current handling hides it. Making checksum failures visible catches both supply-chain tampering and accidental build/packaging corruption that would otherwise ship unnoticed.

title: Fix stale-response race in dynamic question options
description: useDynamicQuestionOptions.ts fires discovery requests on parent-answer change, but requestIdRef stale-checks only guard setStateByQuestion; they do not cancel or stale-check the questionnaire submit path. If the user changes a depends_on parent answer and clicks Submit before the new discovery resolves, the submission may bind stale option values. Add (a) an AbortController for each discovery call cancelled on answer change, (b) a pending-discovery guard on Submit that either waits or re-validates the current answers.
reasoning: This produces silently-wrong personas. The adoption wizard appears to work but binds the prior connector or resource selection. Users only notice later when the persona fails in execution. Cancellation plus submit-gating is a small, local fix to a high-impact correctness bug.

title: Distinguish empty-catalog from loading-failed in UI
description: templateCatalog.loadAndVerify() returns [] both when the catalog legitimately has no published templates and when all templates were filtered by failed checks. Gallery code cannot tell these apart, so users see an indistinguishable blank state. Return a discriminated result (status: ok|partial|failed, templates, skipped: [{id, reason}]) and render distinct UI: empty-state illustration vs. error with retry vs. partial-load warning listing skipped template ids.
reasoning: Today a broken catalog looks identical to an empty catalog. Support cannot triage, and users get no actionable signal. Surfacing partial-load state also exposes checksum failures as they happen, not via Sentry days later.

title: Cleanup adoption wizard state when modal closes mid-transform
description: AdoptionWizardModal calls resetBuildSession() on open but has no teardown when the modal closes while backgroundTransformId is still running. The Rust-side snapshot and zustand wizard state persist, so reopening the same template reattaches stale answers and partial questionnaire progress. Add an onClose effect that (a) cancels or detaches from the in-flight transform, (b) clears wizard answers and backgroundTransformId, (c) marks the build session terminated so the backend drops the snapshot.
reasoning: Users hit this anytime they start adoption, get distracted, close, and later retry the same template. They see a half-answered questionnaire and cannot tell why. It also leaks backend resources for abandoned transforms. Low-risk, localized fix.

title: Log template id context when overlay is skipped
description: templateOverlays.ts:190-194 warns 'Overlay missing id field, skipping' with only {path}. Missing: the canonical template id, the overlay locale, and the merged field list. Translations silently vanish for that locale+template pair with no correlation in logs. Expand the warn to include {path, locale, expectedTemplateId, overlayKeys}, and add a Sentry breadcrumb so per-locale regressions surface in dashboards.
reasoning: Localization bugs are the hardest to catch in QA since reviewers rarely switch the 14 supported languages. Richer log context lets the i18n team correlate user reports with specific overlay issues without needing to reproduce.

title: Detect duplicate use_case IDs across template catalog
description: templateCatalog loads templates without validating that use_case ids are unique across the catalog. Multiple templates share ids like uc_triage, and downstream code in the adoption wizard resolves questions by use_case_id alone, producing cross-template capability contamination. Add a post-load validator that (a) asserts intra-template use_case id uniqueness, (b) detects cross-template collisions and either namespaces them (template_id:use_case_id) or raises a dev-mode hard error. Run the validator as a vitest unit test against the real catalog so new templates cannot ship a colliding id.
reasoning: This is the same failure class flagged by the capability-id collision idea, applied to use_cases. A single missed collision silently routes the adoption wizard to the wrong questions, creating broken personas. A test-time guardrail is the only durable fix.

title: Surface JSON parse failures in template generation snapshot
description: useCreateTemplateSnapshot.ts:20-24 wraps JSON.parse(snap.result_json) in a silent try/catch and returns a null persona draft on failure. Users see 'completed with no result' with no diagnostic. Replace the silent fallback with (a) toastCatch surfacing a friendly parse error, (b) Sentry capture carrying the first 500 scrubbed chars of result_json and the template id, (c) a retry affordance that re-requests generation. Consolidate scope with sibling idea a0e0fcbf.
reasoning: A backend that corrupts its own JSON is never self-healing, and today it is indistinguishable from a slow generation. Surfacing the parse failure turns a mystery into an actionable bug and prevents the user from waiting forever or retrying blindly.
